rule: No source entry = no published claim. Sources are added to this registry only when verified. No placeholder or fabricated URLs are permitted.

sources.title: bisulfide
use_for: English lexical entry for bisulfide
cautious terminology and spelling context
first-known-use or etymology context only if directly supported by the source
risk_notes: Dictionary source only; not a chemical safety, regulatory, industrial, or market authority.
Do not use to validate handling, procurement, exposure, medical, or market claims.
sources.notes: Merriam-Webster dictionary entry for 'bisulfide'. Establishes English-language lexical existence of the term.

sources.title: sulfide

cautious terminology and spelling context
first-known-use or etymology context only if directly supported by the source
risk_notes: Dictionary source only; not a chemical safety, regulatory, industrial, or market authority.
Do not use to validate handling, procurement, exposure, medical, or market claims.
sources.notes: Merriam-Webster dictionary entry for 'sulfide'. Establishes English-language lexical existence of the parent chemical family term.

sources.title: bisulfite
use_for: English lexical entry for bisulfite
disambiguation between bisulfite and bisulfide
evidence that bisulfite is a separate lexical entry from bisulfide
risk_notes: Use only for disambiguation.
Must never be used as evidence for bisulfide, hydrosulfide, or sulfide claims.
sources.notes: Merriam-Webster dictionary entry for 'bisulfite'. Supports disambiguation: bisulfite is a distinct compound from bisulfide. Required for bisulfite_disambiguation term record.

sources.title: Sulfid
use_for: German spelling confirmation for Sulfid
German-language term identity
linguistic support for the German identity layer
risk_notes: German dictionary source only; not a chemical safety, industrial, or market authority.
Supports linguistic identity, not industrial proof.
sources.notes: Duden dictionary entry for 'Sulfid'. Establishes German-language lexical existence of the -id suffix form. Supports the sulfid term record and the German-English spelling boundary thesis.

sources.title: Oxid — Rechtschreibung, Bedeutung, Definition, Herkunft
use_for: German lexical entry for Oxid
candidate support that Oxid is a German chemical-language form
cautious example in the Oxide/Oxid spelling comparison
risk_notes: Dictionary source only; supports lexical form, not formal nomenclature authority.
Do not use alone to claim a universal German -ide to -id rule.
sources.notes: Duden dictionary entry for 'Oxid'. Candidate lexical support only for cautious German-English spelling comparison.

sources.notes: Duden dictionary entry for 'Chlorid'. Candidate lexical support only for cautious German-English spelling comparison.

sources.title: Nomenclature of Inorganic Chemistry
use_for: English inorganic nomenclature context
cautious support for English -ide naming context where directly stated
comparison context for English terms such as chloride and sulfide
risk_notes: Historical IUPAC nomenclature source; useful for English nomenclature context only.
Must not be used to prove German spelling compression.
sources.notes: IUPAC inorganic nomenclature material. Candidate English nomenclature context only; not a German-language suffix authority.

sources.title: hydrosulfides
use_for: controlled nomenclature context for hydrosulfides
source-locked explanation of IUPAC terminology context
terminology relationship between hydrosulfide/hydrosulfides where directly supported
risk_notes: Nomenclature source only.
Do not convert nomenclature context into safety, medical, industrial, or market claims.
sources.notes: IUPAC Gold Book entry for hydrosulfides. Authoritative chemical nomenclature standard establishing the hydrosulfide term and its relationship to bisulfide chemistry.

sources.title: Hydrosulfide
use_for: chemical identity context for hydrosulfide
formula or database identity where directly supported by PubChem
candidate support for HS- identity references
risk_notes: Database identity source; not a substitute for safety guidance or regulatory advice.
Do not use hazard fields, if present, as prescriptive handling instructions.
sources.notes: PubChem (NIH) compound record for Hydrosulfide. Government scientific database entry supporting chemical identity claim.

sources.title: Sodium hydrosulfide
use_for: chemical identity context for sodium hydrosulfide / NaHS
formula or database identity where directly supported by PubChem
synonym context only if directly supported by PubChem fields
do_not_use_for: final sodium bisulfide equivalence without additional source-locking
risk_notes: Candidate support only for sodium hydrosulfide / NaHS identity.
Mapping to sodium bisulfide remains candidate and requires dedicated source review before publication.
sources.notes: PubChem (NIH) compound record for Sodium hydrosulfide (NaHS). Supports both sodium_hydrosulfide and sodium_bisulfide term records as candidate synonym/identity source.

sources.title: Hydrogen sulfide
use_for: chemical identity context for hydrogen sulfide
formula and molecular identity where directly supported by NIST
risk_notes: Chemistry database source; not a safety guidance source.
Do not use to generate handling or emergency advice.
sources.notes: NIST Chemistry WebBook entry for Hydrogen sulfide (H2S, CAS 7783-06-4). Government scientific database supporting hydrogen_sulfide term record.

sources.title: Messung von Bisulfid und Schwefelwasserstoff im Wasser
use_for: candidate German technical usage of the term Bisulfid
candidate support for Bisulfid appearing in a German water-quality / sulfur-monitoring context
candidate context connecting Bisulfid with HS- / H₂S terminology where directly stated by the source
do_not_use_for: proof that Bisulfid is the preferred German IUPAC form
final equivalence between Bisulfid and hydrosulfide without additional source-locking
risk_notes: Commercial technical source only; not a dictionary, IUPAC source, regulatory source, or government scientific database.
Can support observed German technical usage, but not final terminology authority.
Any safety or monitoring content on the page must not be turned into public safety advice.
sources.notes: Badger Meter German-language technical page on measuring Bisulfid and Schwefelwasserstoff (HS⁻ / H₂S) in water. Supports observed German technical usage of the term Bisulfid in a water-quality monitoring context. Highest-category source available for bisulfid center node at sprint 3B; stronger authoritative source (dictionary, nomenclature standard, regulatory database) still required.
